My audit log channel where I send server actions and events (e.g. rankgs and expiring roles).

If configured, I will log most significant actions taken or events which occur through my interface, into this channel. Logged events include, for example:
- Member voice activity
- Roles equipped and expiring from rolemenus
- Privated rooms rented and expiring
- Activity ranks earned
I must have the 'Manage Webhooks' permission in this channel.

See a brief summary of my commands and features.
Invite LionBot to your own server.
[Click here]({invite_link}) to add me to your server.

Remember to check out {help_cmd} for the important command list, including the admin page which displays the hidden admin-level configuration commands like {dashboard}!
Also, if you have any issues or questions, you can join our [support server]({support_link}) to talk to our friendly support team!
View hidden details and statistics about me ('nerd statistics')
`{people}` people active in `{rooms}` rooms of `{guilds}` guilds.
`{hours}` voice hours from `{users}` people across `{sessions}` sessions.
Have an issue or a question? Speak to my friendly support team here.

Speak to my friendly support team by joining this server and making a ticket in the support channel!
{support_link}

{config_emoji} Personal Configuration
    *View or adjust personal settings with the {cmd_my} command.*
    {cmd_my_timezone}: Timezone used to display your stats and set reminders.
    {cmd_my_language}: Your preferred language for commands and interactions.


    {stats_emoji} Statistics
    *Study in voice channels to earn activity ranks and compete on the leaderboard!*
    {cmd_me}: View your personal study profile and set your profile tags.
    {cmd_stats}: View study statistics for the current and past weeks or months.
    {cmd_ranks}: See the list of activity ranks.
    {cmd_leaderboard}: Compete with other members on the server leaderboards.


    {coin} Economy
    *Earn coins through studying, then spend them on some well deserved rewards!*
    {cmd_send}: Send your {coin} to another member.
    {cmd_shop}: Purchase server roles with your {coin}.
    {cmd_room}: Rent a private voice channel for you and your friends.


    {utility_emoji} Utilities
    *Some other utilities to help you stay productive while studying!*
    {cmd_reminders}: Ask me to remind you about that important task!
    {cmd_tasklist}: Create tasks and feel the satisfaction of checking them off.
    {cmd_timers}: Stay productive using the classic *pomodoro technique*!
    {cmd_schedule}: Schedule a shared study session and keep yourself accountable!

Use {cmd_dashboard} to see an overview of the server configuration,     and quickly jump to the feature configuration panels to modify settings.

    Most settings may also be directly set through the `/config` and `/admin config` commands,     depending on whether the settings require moderator (manage server) or admin level permissions, respectively.

    Other relevant commands for guild configuration below:
    `/editshop`: Add/Edit/Remove colour roles from the {coin} shop.
    `/ranks`: Add/Edit/Refresh/Remove activity ranks.
    `/pomodoro`: Add/Edit/Remove Pomodoro timers in voice channels.
    `/rolemenus`: Allow members to equip roles from customisable messages.
    `/economy balance`: Display and modify LionCoin balance for members and roles.

Thank you for inviting me to your community!
Get started by typing {help_cmd} to see my commands, and {dash_cmd} to view and set up my configuration options!

If you need any help configuring me, or would like to suggest a feature, report a bug, and stay updated, make sure to join our main support server by [clicking here]({support}).

The reason why you are warning this member.
Server role allowing access to all administrator level functionality in Leo.

Members with this role are considered to be server administrators, allowing them to use all of my interfaces and commands, except for managing roles that are above them in the role hierachy. This setting allows giving members administrator-level permissions over my systems, without actually giving the members admin server permissions. Note that the role will also need to be given permission to see the commands through the Discord server integrations interface.
Members with {role} will now be considered admins, and have access to my full interface.
The admin role has been unset. Only members with administrator permissions will be considered admins.
Moderation notification channel for members with DMs disabled.
Not Set (Only alert via direct message.)

When I need to send a member a moderation-related notification (e.g. asking them to enable their video in a video channel) from this server, I will try to send it via direct messages. If this fails, I will instead mention the user in this channel.
Moderation alerts will be sent to {channel}
Moderation alerts will be ignored if the member cannot be reached.
Server role permitted to perform moderation and minor bot configuration.

Members with the moderator role are considered moderators, and are permitted to use moderator commands, such as viewing and pardoning moderation tickets, creating moderation notes, and performing minor reconfiguration through the `/config` command.
Moderators are never permitted to perform actions (such as giving roles) that they do not already have the Discord permissions for.
Members with the 'Manage Guild' permission are always considered moderators.
Members with {role} will be considered moderators. You may need to grant them access to view moderation commands via the server integration settings.
Only members with the 'Manage Guild' permission will be considered moderators.
Private moderation log to send tickets and moderation events.

Which statistic is used to reward activity ranks.
`Voice` is the number of hours active in tracked voice channels, `Exp` is a measure of message activity, and `Message` is a simple count of messages sent.

- Use {in_cmd} to set a reminder in a certain time (e.g. `in 2 hours`).

Base command group for role menu configuration.
Custom message data used to display the menu.

This setting determines the body of the menu message, including the message content and the message embed(s). While most easily modifiable through the `Edit Message` button, raw JSON-formatted message data may also be uploaded via command.
The role menu message has been set. Edit through the menu editor.
The role menu message has been unset. Select a template through the menu editor.
Brief name for this role menu.
The role menu name is displayed when selecting the menu in commands, and as the title of most default menu layouts.
This role menu will now be called **{new_name}**.
The maximum number of roles equippable from this menu.
Members will not be able to obtain more than this number of roles from this menu. This counts roles that were not obtained through the rolemenu system.
Members will be able to select a maximum of **{value}** roles from this menu.
Members will be able to select any number of roles from this menu.
Whether removing a role will refund the purchase price for that role.

When enabled, members who *purchased a role through this role menu* will obtain a full refund when they remove the role through the menu.
**Refunds will only be given for roles purchased through the same menu.**
**The `sticky` option must be disabled to allow members to remove roles.**
Members will no longer be refunded when removing a role with this menu.
Members will now be refunded when removing a role with this menu.
Initial role required to use this menu.
If set, only members who have the `required_role` will be able to obtain or remove roles using this menu.
Members will need to have the {role} role to use this menu.
Any member who can see the menu may use it.
Whether the menu can be used to unequip roles.

When enabled, members will not be able to remove equipped roles by selecting them in this menu. Note that when disabled, members will be able to unequip the menu roles even if they were not obtained from the menu.
Members will now be able to remove roles with this menu.
Members will no longer be able to remove roles with this menu.

Bonus coins rewarded when everyone attends a session.
Bonus reward given when all members attend a scheduled session.
{coin}**{amount}** bonus when all booked members attend.
When all members who have booked a session successfully attend the session, they will be given this bonus in *addition* to the `attendance_reward`.

Number of coins to reward session attendance.
Reward for attending a booked scheduled session.
When a member successfully attends a scheduled session they booked, they will be awarded this many LionCoins. Should generally be more than the `schedule_cost` setting.
Members will be rewarded {coin}**{amount}** when they attend a scheduled session.
Number of minutes (1-60) before attendance is counted.
Scheduled session members will need to attend the session for at least this number of minutes before they are marked as having attended (and hence are rewarded).
Minimum attendance must be an integer number of minutes between `1` and `60`.
Members will be rewarded after they have attended booked sessions for at least **`{amount}`** minutes.
A number of missed sessions (1-24) before blacklisting.
Number of missed sessions within 24h before blacklisting.

Members who miss more than this number of booked sessions in a single 24 hour period will be automatically given the `blacklist_role`. Has no effect if the `blacklist_role` is not set or if I do not have sufficient permissions to assign the blacklist role.
Members will be blacklisted after **`{amount}`** missed sessions within `24h`.
Members will not be automatically blacklisted from booking scheduled sessions.
Role which disables scheduled session booking.
{role} members will not be able to book scheduled sessions.

Members with this role will not be allowed to book scheduled sessions in this server. If the role is manually added, all future scheduled sessions for the user are cancelled. This provides a way to stop repeatedly unreliable members from blocking the group bonus for all members. Alternatively, consider setting the booking cost (and reward) very high to provide a strong disincentive for not attending a session.
Blacklist threshold must be a number between `1` and `24`.
Members with {role} will be unable to book scheduled sessions.
The schedule blacklist role has been unset.
Price of each session booking (non-negative integer).

Voice channels in which to track activity for scheduled sessions.

Only activity in these channels (and in `session_room` if set) will count towards scheduled session attendance. If a category is selected, then all channels under the category will also be included. Activity tracking also respects the `untracked_voice_channels` setting.
Activity in the following sessions will now count towards scheduled session attendance: {channels}
Activity in all (tracked) voice channels will now count towards session attendance.

Channel in which to announce scheduled sessions and post their status. I must have the `MANAGE_WEBHOOKS` permission in this channel.
**This must be configured in order for the scheduled session system to function.**
Scheduled sessions will now be announced in {channel}
The schedule session lobby has been unset. Shutting down scheduled session system.
Name or id of the session room voice channel.
Special voice channel open to scheduled session members.

If set, this voice channel serves as a dedicated room for scheduled session members. During (and slightly before) each scheduled session, all members who have booked the session will be given permission to join the voice channel (via permission overwrites). I require the `MANAGE_CHANNEL`, `MANAGE_PERMISSIONS`, `CONNECT`, and `VIEW_CHANNEL` permissions in this channel, and my highest role must be higher than all permission overwrites set in the channel. Furthermore, if this is set to a *category* channel, then the permission overwrites will apply to all *synced* channels under the category, as usual.
Schedule session members will now be given access to {channel}
The dedicated schedule session room has been removed.

**Members who missed their session have all future sessions cancelled without refund!*
I cancelled this scheduled session because I was unavailable. All members who booked the session have been refunded.

**Do you think you can commit to a schedule and stick to it?**
**Schedule voice sessions here and get rewarded for keeping yourself accountable!**

Use the menu below to book timeslots using LionCoins. If you are active (in the dedicated voice channels) during these times, you will be rewarded, along with a large bonus if everyone who scheduled that slot made it!
Beware though, if you fail to make it, all your booked sessions will be cancelled with no refund! And if you keep failing to attend your scheduled sessions, you may be forbidden from booking them in future.

When your scheduled session starts, you will recieve a ping from the schedule channel, which will have more information about how to attend your session.
If you discover you can't make your scheduled session, please be responsible and use this command to cancel or clear your schedule!

**Note:** *Make sure your timezone is set correctly (with `/my timezone`), or the times I tell might not make sense!*
No upcoming sessions to cancel! Your schedule is already clear.

Channels in which to publicly display member tasklists.

A member's tasklist (from {cmds[tasklist]}) is usually only visible to the member themselves. If set, tasklists opened in `tasklist_channels` will be visible to all members, and the interface will have a much longer expiry period. If a category is provided, this will apply to all channels under the category.

Role given when members are blacklisted from video channels.
Not Set. (Members will not be automatically blacklisted.)

This role will be automatically given after a member has failed to keep their video enabled in a video channel (see above).
Members who have this role will not be able to join configured video channels. The role permissions may be freely configured by server admins to place further restrictions on the offender.
The role may also be manually assigned, to the same effect.
If this role is not set, no video blacklist will occur, and members will only be kicked from the channel and warned.
Members who fail to keep their video on will be given {role}
Members will no longer be automatically blacklisted from video channels.
Comma separated channel ids or names.
List of voice channels and categories in which to enforce video.

Member will be required to turn on their video in these channels.
If they do not enable their video with `15` seconds of joining, they will be asked to enable it through a notification in direct messages or the `alert_channel`. If they still have not enabled it after the `video_grace_period` has passed, they will be kicked from the channel. Further, after the first offence (which is considered a warning), they will be given the `video_blacklist` role, if configured, which will stop them from joining video channels.
As usual, if a category is configured, this will apply to all voice channels under the category.
Members will be asked to turn on their video in the following channels: {channels}
Members will not be asked to turn on their video in any channels.
Sequence of durations for automatic video blacklists.

When `video_blacklist` is set and members fail to turn on their video within the configured `video_grace_period`, they will be automatically blacklisted (i.e. given the `video_blacklist` role).
This setting describes *how long* the member will be blacklisted for, for each offence.
E.g. if this is set to `1d, 7d, 30d`, then on the first offence the member will be blacklisted for 1 day, on the second for 7 days, and on the third for 30 days. A subsequent offence will result in an infinite blacklist.
Members will be automatically blacklisted for: {durations}
Video blacklists are now always permanent.
List of exempt role names or ids.
List of roles which are exempt from video channels.
Members who have **any** of these roles will not be required to enable their video in the `video_channels`. This also overrides the `video_blacklist` role.
The following roles will now be exempt from video channels: {roles}
No members will be exempt from video channel requirements.
How many seconds to wait for a member to enable video.
How long to wait (in seconds) before kicking/blacklist members who don't enable their video.

The length of time a member has to enable their video after joining a video channel. After this time, if they have not enabled their video, they will be kicked from the channel and potentially blacklisted from video channels.
Members will now have **{duration}** to enable their video.
